claims.claim: A toshav chozer vatik (long-term returning resident) is an individual who was a foreign tax resident for at least 10 consecutive years before becoming an Israeli tax resident again.
claims.raw_snippet: תושב חוזר ותיק הוא יחיד שחזר לישראל לאחר ששהה בחו"ל 10 שנים רצופות לפחות והיה במהלכן תושב חוץ.

claims.claim: Section 14 of the Income Tax Ordinance is the statutory basis for the 10-year foreign-income exemption for toshav chozer vatik and olim chadashim.
claims.raw_snippet: תושב חוזר ותיק זכאי לפטור ממס על הכנסות שהופקו או נצמחו מחוץ לישראל, למשך 10 שנים מהיום בו הפך לתושב ישראל. הפטור חל על מכלול ההכנסות, האקטיביות והפסיביות.

claims.claim: Section 14 of the Income Tax Ordinance defines the substantive exemption; the definition of toshav chozer vatik appears in Section 1 of the Ordinance.
claims.raw_snippet: פקודת מס הכנסה (נוסח חדש), הגדרת תושב חוזר ותיק מופיעה בסעיף 1, וההסדר המהותי של הפטור הוא בסעיף 14 לפקודה.

claims.claim: The 10-year exemption clock starts from the date the individual becomes an Israeli tax resident again, not from the date of physical return to Israel.
claims.raw_snippet: ספירת השנים מתחילה במועד בו הפך היחיד לתושב ישראל לצורכי מס. מועד זה עשוי להיות שונה ממועד החזרה הפיזית לארץ.

claims.claim: A regular toshav chozer is an Israeli who was a foreign tax resident for at least 6 consecutive years (or 3 consecutive years if they left Israel before 1 January 2009) before returning.
claims.raw_snippet: תושב חוזר הוא יחיד אשר ניתק את מרכז החיים בישראל לטובת מגורי קבע בחו"ל לפחות 6 שנים רצופות (או 3 שנים אם עזב את ישראל לפני 1.1.2009).

claims.claim: A regular toshav chozer receives a 5-year exemption on passive income (interest, dividends, rentals, royalties) from foreign assets acquired during their period abroad. Active income is NOT exempt.
claims.raw_snippet: פטור ממס למשך 5 שנים על הכנסות פסיביות (ריבית, דיבידנדים, שכר דירה, תמלוגים) מנכסים בחו"ל שנרכשו בתקופת השהות מחוץ לישראל. הכנסה אקטיבית אינה פטורה.

claims.claim: A regular toshav chozer also receives a 10-year capital gains exemption on the sale of foreign assets acquired during the period abroad (provided the asset does not represent rights to Israeli property).
claims.raw_snippet: רווחי הון ממכירת אותם נכסים יהיו פטורים ממס לתקופה של 10 שנים, ובלבד שאין מדובר בזכות לנכס בישראל.

claims.claim: Amendment 272 to the Income Tax Ordinance cancels sections 134b and 135(b), abolishing the reporting exemption for foreign income and assets of toshav chozer vatik and olim chadashim. The tax exemption itself remains.
claims.raw_snippet: תיקון 272, מבטל את סעיפים 134ב ו־135(ב) לפקודה. החל מ־1 בינואר 2026 יבוטל הפטור מדיווח. הפטור ממס עצמו נשאר בתוקף.

claims.claim: The cancellation of the reporting exemption applies only to individuals who become Israeli residents (olim or vatik) on or after 1 January 2026. Those who became residents before 1.1.2026 retain both the tax and reporting exemptions for the original 10-year window.
claims.raw_snippet: החוק יחול רק על תושבים שהפכו לתושבי ישראל לאחר 1 בינואר 2026. ביטול הפטור מדיווח לא יחול על מי שכבר נמצא בישראל או מי שיגיע לישראל עד ליום 1.1.2026.

claims.claim: Post-2026 vatik returnees and olim must report foreign income on Form 1301 + Schedule D-1, plus capital declarations on Tax Authority request, plus trust and CFC disclosures.
claims.raw_snippet: Annual reports on foreign-source income (Schedule D1, Form 1301), Trust notifications and distributions reporting, Capital declarations regarding foreign assets (if requested by tax authorities).

claims.claim: Form 1348 (Hatzharat Toshavut) is filed by Israelis LEAVING Israel who wish to declare non-residency, NOT by returning residents. It is an annex to Form 1301 and challenges the day-count presumption of Israeli residency under the center-of-life test.
claims.raw_snippet: Form 1348 is a residency declaration form used to report non-resident tax status in Israel. The form is specifically for individuals who may appear to meet Israel quantitative residency thresholds but wish to claim they are not Israeli tax residents based on the center of life test.

claims.claim: Israeli banks typically request Form 2409 within 14 days of an incoming foreign-currency deposit to confirm the depositor's toshav chozer status. The 14-day window is bank convention, not statute, and varies by bank.
claims.raw_snippet: תוך 14 ימים מהפקדת כספים מחו"ל, על היחיד להגיש לבנק טופס 2409 המאשר את מעמדו כתושב חוזר.

claims.claim: The Returning Resident Certificate issued by the Ministry of Aliyah and Integration is an administrative document that does NOT grant tax benefits and does NOT bind the Tax Authority. Section 14 status is determined independently by Mas Hachnasa under the Ordinance.
claims.raw_snippet: תעודת תושב חוזר ותיק המוענקת ע"י משרד הקליטה, אינה מקנה הטבות מס מכוח מעמד תושב חוזר כהגדרתו בפקודת מס הכנסה ואינה מחייבת את רשות המסים בעניין תושבות לצורכי מס.

claims.claim: The Section 14(b) year-of-acclimation (shnat hashtagrut) election must be filed within 90 days of arrival in Israel via Form 1130. Miss the window and the deferral is unavailable.
claims.raw_snippet: על יחיד מוטב להודיע על בחירתו בהחלת המנגנון בתוך 90 ימים ממועד הגעתו לישראל. הטופס הוא טופס 1130 - הודעת בחירה לשנת הסתגלות.

claims.claim: ITA Circular 1/2011 (חוזר מס הכנסה 1/2011) provides official guidance on the year-of-acclimation election under Section 14(b), confirming the 90-day deadline and the discretionary late-filing path.
claims.raw_snippet: Official ITA publication: חוזר מס הכנסה 1/2011 dated 18.01.2011 on the year-of-acclimation mechanism under Section 14(b) of the Income Tax Ordinance.

claims.claim: The Israel Tax Authority published a circular in October 2025 describing the implementation of Amendment 272 and the cancellation of the reporting exemption.
claims.raw_snippet: חוזר מס הכנסה רשות המסים אוקטובר 2025 על תיקון 272 וביטול הפטור מדיווח לעולים חדשים ותושבים חוזרים ותיקים.

claims.claim: The Knesset Research and Information Center published an analysis of tax benefits for olim and toshav chozer vatik, including the 2026 reform.
claims.raw_snippet: תיאור וניתוח הטבות מס לעולים חדשים ולתושבים חוזרים, מרכז המחקר והמידע של הכנסת.

claims.claim: Form 1348 is officially published by the Israeli Tax Authority as a residency-declaration annex.
claims.raw_snippet: official ITA publication of Form 1348 residency declaration annex to annual report Form 1301.

claims.claim: BSH CPA practitioner guide on Form 1348 confirms it is used by Israelis leaving Israel claiming non-residency.
claims.raw_snippet: טופס 1348 מוגש על ידי תושב ישראל שביצע תהליך רילוקיישן ומעוניין לטעון כי אינו תושב ישראל.

claims.claim: The 2026 Hok Iddud Aliyah veChazara (Temporary Provision), 5786-2026, sets annual caps on Israeli-source qualifying income exemption: 600,000 NIS in 2026, 1,000,000 NIS in 2027 and 2028, 350,000 NIS in 2029, 150,000 NIS in 2030.
claims.raw_snippet: עד לתקרת הכנסה של 600,000 שקלים חדשים בשנת 2026, עד לתקרת הכנסה של מיליון שקלים חדשים, לכל שנה בשנים 2027 ו-2028, עד לתקרת הכנסה של 350,000 שקלים חדשים בשנת 2029, עד לתקרת הכנסה של 150,000 שקלים חדשים בשנת 2030.

claims.claim: The 2026 Hok Iddud cap on Israeli-source labor income is 600,000 NIS in 2026, proportional to the period of Israeli residency during that year.
claims.raw_snippet: פטור על הכנסה מזכה שהופקה או נצמחה בישראל בתקרות: עד 600,000 ש"ח בשנת 2026, באופן יחסי לתקופת התושבות בישראל באותה שנה.

claims.claim: The 2026 Hok Iddud caps for years 2027 and 2028 are 1,000,000 NIS each.
claims.raw_snippet: עד 1,000,000 ש"ח בכל אחת משנות המס 2027 ו-2028.

claims.claim: The 2026 Hok Iddud cap for 2029 is 350,000 NIS.
The 2026 Hok Iddud cap for 2030 is 150,000 NIS.
The US-Israel Income Tax Treaty 1995 includes a saving clause (Article 6, paragraph 3) that allows the United States to tax its citizens and residents as if the treaty had not come into effect.
claims.raw_snippet: a Contracting State may tax its residents and its citizens as if this Convention had not come into effect

claims.claim: The United States taxes its citizens on worldwide income regardless of where they reside.
claims.raw_snippet: the U.S. imposes taxes on citizenship and worldwide income

claims.claim: The official US-Israel Income Tax Treaty 1995 text is published by the IRS.
claims.raw_snippet: Official IRS publication of the Convention between the Government of the United States of America and the Government of the State of Israel with respect to taxes on income.

claims.claim: The Israeli Tax Authority sources income from stock options and RSUs by the place where the work was performed during the vesting period of the options, not by grant date.
claims.raw_snippet: The place of production of income in this case will be determined according to the place where the work was performed during the vesting period of the options (i.e., part of the income may be classified as 'Israeli' and the balance as income produced abroad).

claims.claim: Under section 92 of the Income Tax Ordinance and ITA Circular 10/2025, capital losses incurred during the toshav chozer vatik exemption period are not deductible against later post-window gains. The matching principle (הקבלה) holds that a loss is offsettable only if a corresponding gain from the same transaction would have been Israeli-taxable.
claims.raw_snippet: הפסדים שנוצרו לעולים חדשים או לתושבים חוזרים ותיקים בתקופת ההטבות לפי סעיפים 14 ו־97(ב) לפקודה אינם ניתנים לקיזוז, שכן אילו היה נוצר רווח הוא היה פטור ממס.

claims.claim: ITA Circular 10/2025 (חוזר מס הכנסה 10/2025), published 27 November 2025, clarifies the rules for offsetting capital losses under section 92, including treatment during the vatik and oleh exemption periods.
claims.raw_snippet: חוזר מס הכנסה מס' 10/2025 בנושא קיזוז הפסדי הון לפי סעיף 92 לפקודת מס הכנסה, פורסם 27 בנובמבר 2025.

claims.claim: Vatik status requires at least 10 consecutive years of foreign tax residency before the return; a center-of-life break during the window (such as a multi-month return to Israel where family and primary home shift back) can disqualify vatik status.
claims.raw_snippet: תושב חוזר ותיק הוא יחיד ששב לישראל לאחר שניתק את תושבות המס שלו והיה תושב חוץ במשך לפחות 10 שנים רצופות.

claims.claim: US citizens living abroad file annual Form 1040, and depending on facts may also file Form 8938 (FATCA, foreign financial assets above the abroad-living threshold of $200,000 single / $400,000 joint), FBAR (FinCEN 114, for combined foreign accounts above $10,000), Form 5471 (controlled foreign corporations), and Form 3520 (foreign trusts and large foreign gifts).
claims.raw_snippet: If you are single or file separately from your spouse, you must submit a Form 8938 if you have more than $200,000 of specified foreign financial assets at the end of the year and you live abroad. Form 1040 is the US federal income tax return. Form 5471 typically applies to US taxpayers with foreign corporation interests; Form 3520 relates to foreign trusts and gifts. FBAR is filed using FinCEN Form 114 when combined foreign accounts exceed $10,000.

claims.claim: The IRS publishes a Technical Explanation of the US-Israel Income Tax Treaty alongside the treaty text itself.
claims.raw_snippet: Official IRS Technical Explanation of the Convention between the United States and Israel with respect to taxes on income, accompanying the treaty PDF at irs.gov/pub/irs-trty.

claims.claim: Lexology / Pearl Cohen practitioner commentary on ITA equity award guidance for mobile employees confirms that the place of work during the vesting period determines income sourcing for cross-border RSU/option grants.
claims.raw_snippet: Lexology article on Israeli Tax Authority clarifies taxation of equity awards held by mobile employees, including sourcing based on place of work during the vesting period.

claims.claim: Israel taxes individual real capital gains on the sale of shares purchased on or after 1 January 2003 at 25%, with a 30% rate for substantial holders (10% or more).
claims.raw_snippet: The real gain realized upon the sale of shares purchased on or after 1 January 2003 is generally taxed at a rate of 25% for individuals. However, this rate is increased to 30% where the seller was a 10% or more shareholder.

claims.claim: Israel taxes individual dividends from Israeli companies at 25% (30% for substantial holders holding 10% or more).
claims.raw_snippet: The tax rate on dividends is 25%, but if the individual receiving the dividend is a significant shareholder in the distributing company (holds 10% or more of the company's share capital), the tax rate to be imposed on the dividends at the time of distribution is 30%.

claims.claim: Section 102 of the Israeli Income Tax Ordinance establishes the trustee track for taxation of employee stock options and equity, with favorable 25% capital gains treatment under the capital-gains route subject to a 24-month trustee holding requirement.
claims.raw_snippet: Section 102 of the Israeli Income Tax Ordinance offers a trustee track for employee stock options. Under the capital gains route, any gain will be taxed at a reduced flat rate of 25%. The trustee must hold the options and exercised shares for at least 24 months from the end of the tax year in which the options were granted.

claims.claim: Section 92 of the Israeli Income Tax Ordinance governs capital loss offsetting. Under the principle of symmetry, capital losses incurred during the toshav chozer vatik / oleh chadash exemption period are dead losses that cannot offset later taxable gains, because the corresponding gain would have been tax-exempt.
claims.raw_snippet: A capital loss created from the sale of an asset abroad during the exemption period is a dead loss that cannot be offset against taxable profits because had a profit been created, it would have been tax-exempt (the Principle of Symmetry). Section 92 of the Ordinance covers the implementation of capital loss offsetting.

claims.claim: Form 1301 (טופס 1301) is the annual tax return for individuals in Israel, filed each year by Israeli residents subject to filing obligations.
claims.raw_snippet: Annual reports on foreign-source income (Schedule D1, Form 1301).

claims.claim: The Israeli Tax Authority publishes a target-audience portal page for returning residents.
claims.raw_snippet: תושבים חוזרים | רשות המסים בישראל. Official ITA portal page for returning residents at gov.il. Verified live via Playwright 2026-05-23 (curl returns 403 due to bot blocking; browser loads fine).

claims.claim: Shivat Zion publishes a plain-English explainer of the 10-year tax exemption for olim and qualifying returning residents.
claims.raw_snippet: an exemption for ten years on reporting and paying taxes on all non-Israeli sourced income. New Olim (new immigrants); Qualifying returning residents (toshav chozer vatik).

claims.claim: Shivat Zion publishes a plain-English overview of the Toshav Chozer status.
claims.raw_snippet: Toshav Chozer (Returning Resident) Overview. Available advantages include: Reduced taxes on household goods imports, Business startup assistance, Income tax benefits, Acclimatization Year (Shnat Histaglut), Employment and training support.

claims.claim: Eligibility for the Hok Iddud 5-year Israeli-source labor exemption is forfeited if the returnee spends fewer than 75 days in Israel during 2028 or fewer than 75 days during 2029.
claims.raw_snippet: You spend fewer than 75 days in Israel during 2028 and 2029 respectively

claims.claim: Income from labor for an immediate family member (parent, spouse, sibling, child, in-law) is capped at 140,000 NIS per year under Hok Iddud, separately and lower than the general 600K/1M annual caps.
claims.raw_snippet: Up to 140,000 NIS per year (for all years 2026-2030)

claims.claim: The Hok Iddud Israeli-source exemption does NOT cover rental income, accrued interest, or dividends; these remain fully taxable Israeli-side.
claims.raw_snippet: Rental income, Accrued interest, Dividends

claims.claim: Hok Iddud annual exemption caps for 2026-2030 are: 2026 up to 600,000 NIS, 2027-2028 up to 1,000,000 NIS per year, 2029 up to 350,000 NIS, 2030 up to 150,000 NIS.

Vatik returnees and olim are entitled to 1-3 additional tax credit points (nekudot zikui) for approximately 4.5 years from return/aliyah, worth approximately 3,000-9,000 NIS per year in reduced Israeli tax.
claims.raw_snippet: 1-3 tax credit points for approximately 4.5 years (Worth approximately 3,000-9,000 NIS per year in tax savings)

claims.claim: The 10-year foreign capital gains exemption for vatik and oleh lives in Section 97(b3) of the Income Tax Ordinance, distinct from Section 14 which covers ordinary foreign income.
claims.raw_snippet: סעיף 97(ב3) לפקודת מס הכנסה קובע פטור מרווח הון לעולה חדש או לתושב חוזר ותיק על מכירת נכס בחו"ל שנרכש לפני שהפך לתושב ישראל, וזאת למשך עשר שנים מהיום בו הפך לתושב.

claims.claim: Amendment 272 imposes a new reporting obligation on Israeli-resident trustees of foreign trusts whose beneficiaries are vatik or oleh, separately from beneficiary-level reporting and applicable even when the trust itself remains exempt.
claims.raw_snippet: new reporting obligation for Israeli-resident trustees regarding trusts not required to file

claims.claim: Belong publishes a Toshav Chozer rights and benefits overview covering tax tiers (regular vs Vatik 10+), Acclimation Year, 10-year exemptions on foreign income, and vehicle import benefits.
claims.raw_snippet: Long-Term Toshav Chozer (Vatik): more than 6 years abroad. Substantial benefits comparable to new Olim. 10-year exemption on overseas income/assets reporting.
